memoryArtifacts.description: Origin Displayed: English - Spelling variations of this family name include: Shelton, Sheltone and others. First found in Yorkshire where they were seated from early times and their first records appeared on the census rolls taken by the ancient Kings of Britain to determine the rate of taxation of their subjects. Some of the first settlers of this family name or some of its variants were: Richard and Francis Shelton who settled in Virginia in 1638; Samuel and Esther Shelton settled in Boston Mass. in 1765 with Hannah; Samuel Shelton settled in the Barbados with his servants in 1680. (From www.HouseOfNames.com Archives copyright  2000 - 2009). This interesting surname, with variant spellings Shilton and Skelton is found widespread in the county of Nottinghamshire. The name itself is of English habitational origin from any of the places so called in Nottinghamshire, Bedfordshire, Norfolk, Shropshire and Staffordshire. The first of these was recorded "Sceltun", in the Domesday Book of 1086, while this source also records the other place names "Eseltone", "Sceltuna", "Saltone", and "Scelfitone", respectively. The place name is composed of the elements "scylf", the Old English word for shelf, ledge, or bank and "tun", the Old English word describing a settlement or enclosure. One John de Schelton appeared in 1260 in the Assize Court Rolls of Yorkshire, while the Assize Court Rolls of Warwickshire record a John Shelton in 1385. Notable name bearers include one Thomas Shelton (died 1620) who was the first translator of "Don Quixote" into English and one Sir Richard Shelton (died 1647) employed by the Duke of Buckingham and appointed solicitor-general (1625-1634) becoming M.P. for Bridgnorth (1626-1628) and treasurer of the Inner Temple in 1628. The first recorded spelling of the family name is shown to be that of Robert de Schelton, which was dated 1191, in the Pipe Rolls of Staffordshire, during the reign of King Richard 1st, known as "The Lionheart", 1189 - 1199. Surnames became necessary when governments introduced personal taxation. In England this was known as Poll Tax. Throughout the centuries, surnames in every country have continued to "develop" often leading to astonishing variants of the original spelling.  Copyright: Name Origin Research www.surnamedb.com 1980 – 2014.

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Shelton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Nottinghamshire): habitational name primarily from Shelton (Nottinghamshire), but also from Shelton (Bedfordshire, Norfolk, Shropshire, and Staffordshire). The placenames all derive from Old English scelf ‘rock, ledge, shelf’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’.

nameMeanings.name: Ralph
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Norman French name, Raulf, a contracted form of the Germanic personal name Radulf, derived from rād ‘counsel’ + wulf ‘wolf’. The spelling with -ph is due to classical influence in the 18th century.

variants.name: Ralf
meanings: From a Norman French name, Raulf, a contracted form of the Germanic personal name Radulf, derived from rād ‘counsel’ + wulf ‘wolf’. The spelling with -ph is due to classical influence in the 18th century.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Ralph De Shelton III was born in 1315, in Shelton, Norfolk, England, his father, John de Shelton, was 68 and his mother, Margaret Brus, was 60. He married Anne De Burgullion about 1339, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 17 November 1378, in Shelton, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 63, and was buried in Shelton, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Ralph De Shelton III est né en 1315, à Shelton, Norfolk, Angleterre, son père, John de Shelton, avait 68 ans et sa mère, Margaret Brus, avait 60 ans. Il a épousé Anne De Burgullion environ 1339, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 17 novembre 1378, à Shelton, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 63 ans, et a été enterré à Shelton, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ko.lifeSummary: Ralph De Shelton III(이)가 1315년 영국 노르폴크 셸턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John de Shelton(은)는 68세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Brus(은)는 60세였다. 그는 대략 1339년 영국 잉글랜드에서 Anne De Burgullion(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1378년 11월 17일 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 셸턴에서 63세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 셸턴에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Ralph De Shelton III родился в 1315 г. в местности шелтон, норфолк, Англия, его отцу по имени John de Shelton было 68 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Brus, 60. Он женился на женщине по имени Anne De Burgullion около 1339 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер 17 ноября 1378 г. в местности шелтон, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 63 лет и был похоронен в местности шелтон, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания.
